title: Pot odds — breakeven equity to call (rake-agnostic, 100bb effective)
bodyMarkdown:
Facing a bet B into pot P, the call needs at least B/(P+B+B) equity vs the betting range to break even. Equivalent to: cost-to-call divided by total pot after the call. Rake-agnostic at 100bb effective; pure pot-odds derivation, no implied odds adjustment.

Worked examples (P = current pot before the bet):
- Bet P (pot-size): need 33% equity. 1 / 3 = 0.333.
- Bet P/2 (half-pot): need 25% equity. 0.5 / 2.0 = 0.25.
- Bet P/3 (third-pot): need 20% equity. 0.333 / 1.667 = 0.20.
- Bet 2P (overbet): need 40% equity. 2 / 5 = 0.40.

This is the floor; villain's range, position, and runout structure determine whether equity reaches it. See POKER_THEORY.md §6.3 for the symmetric breakeven-bluff form.
theoryCitation: POKER_THEORY.md §6.3 (Breakeven Bluff Frequency — pot odds is the symmetric form from the calling side)

assumptions.field: all 9-handed live cash and tournament fields (formula is field-invariant)

atomicityJustification: Single derivation (pot odds breakeven) presented as one card.
